0_Overview: What is C++: General-purpose, Multi-paradigm, Compiled Language
Benefits: Maximum Performance — Close to Hardware
Benefits: Zero-overhead Abstractions
Benefits: Huge Ecosystem and Legacy Codebase
Benefits: Full Control Over Memory
Benefits: Used in Game Engines, OS, Browsers, Databases
Benefits: Supports OOP, Generic, Functional, Procedural Paradigms
Drawbacks: Complex Language — Many Ways to Do the Same Thing
Drawbacks: Manual Memory Management — Prone to Bugs (use smart pointers)
Drawbacks: Undefined Behavior is Dangerous
Drawbacks: Slow Compilation
Drawbacks: No Built-in Package Manager (CMake, Conan, vcpkg)
Drawbacks: Header Files and Build System Complexity
When to Use: Game Development (Unreal Engine)
When to Use: Systems Programming, OS, Drivers
When to Use: High-frequency Trading, Real-time Systems
When to Use: Embedded Systems with More Resources than C
When to Use: Graphics, Rendering, Physics Engines
When NOT to Use: Web backends, Scripting, Data Science (use Python)
C++ vs C: OOP, Templates, STL, RAII
C++ vs Rust: Safety vs Ecosystem Maturity
C++ vs Java: Performance vs Portability
1_Fundamentals: Compilation Process: Preprocessor → Compiler → Assembler → Linker
Namespaces: namespace, using namespace, Nested Namespaces
Data Types: int, long, long long, float, double, char, bool, void
Type Modifiers: signed, unsigned, short, long
2_OOP: Classes and Objects: class vs struct (access default)
Copy Semantics: Copy Constructor, Copy Assignment
Move Semantics: Move Constructor, Move Assignment (C++11)
Pure Virtual Functions and Abstract Classes
3_Modern_Cpp: C++11: auto, range-for, lambda, nullptr, move semantics, smart pointers
C++11: initializer_list, uniform initialization {}, constexpr, noexcept
C++14: Generic Lambdas, Return Type Deduction, std::make_unique
C++17: Structured Bindings, if constexpr, std::optional, std::variant, std::any
C++17: Fold Expressions, Class Template Argument Deduction (CTAD)
Lambda Expressions: Capture List, Mutable, Generic
std::span<T>: Non-owning View of Contiguous Data
4_Templates_and_Metaprogramming: SFINAE: Substitution Failure Is Not An Error
5_STL: Containers: vector, deque, list, forward_list, array
Iterators: Input, Output, Forward, Bidirectional, Random Access
Algorithms: sort, find, count, transform, accumulate, copy, remove, unique
Ranges (C++20): views::filter, views::transform, views::take, Lazy Evaluation
6_Memory_Management: new and delete — Manual Memory Management
Memory Leaks and How to Detect Them
std::shared_ptr: Shared Ownership with Reference Counting
make_unique and make_shared — Prefer Over new
